sources.name: ESA Gaia Archive
sources.notes: Star fields mirror Gaia/SIMBAD-style position, distance, brightness, and proper motion attributes. Live source cache merged when available.

sources.name: NASA Exoplanet Archive TAP
sources.notes: Exoplanet fields align with confirmed planet and host-star parameters. Live source cache merged when available.

sources.name: NASA/JPL SBDB Close-Approach Data API
sources.notes: NEO records use close-approach date, distance, velocity, diameter, and hazard flag. Live source cache merged when available.

sources.name: SIMBAD Astronomical Database
sources.notes: Deep sky records use SIMBAD-style identifiers, object classes, positions, distances, and constellation context. Live source cache merged when available.

deepSkyObjects.name: Orion Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A nearby stellar nursery where new stars are forming inside glowing gas.

deepSkyObjects.name: Pleiades
deepSkyObjects.summary: A bright young open cluster whose blue stars are visible without a telescope.

deepSkyObjects.name: Hyades
deepSkyObjects.summary: The nearest major open cluster and a useful benchmark for stellar distances.

deepSkyObjects.name: Andromeda Galaxy
deepSkyObjects.summary: The nearest large spiral galaxy, far outside the local stellar neighborhood.

deepSkyObjects.name: Crab Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A supernova remnant whose expanding gas records a stellar explosion observed in 1054.

deepSkyObjects.name: Hercules Globular Cluster
deepSkyObjects.summary: A dense halo cluster containing hundreds of thousands of old stars.

deepSkyObjects.name: Eagle Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A star-forming region that contains the Pillars of Creation.

deepSkyObjects.name: Omega Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A massive emission nebula sculpted by young hot stars.

deepSkyObjects.name: Trifid Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A compact nebula where dark lanes divide glowing gas into three lobes.

deepSkyObjects.name: Dumbbell Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A nearby planetary nebula formed as a dying Sun-like star shed its outer layers.

deepSkyObjects.name: Ring Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A planetary nebula with a ring-like shell around a hot stellar remnant.

deepSkyObjects.name: Lagoon Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A bright emission nebula and star-forming region visible in the summer Milky Way.

deepSkyObjects.name: Bode's Galaxy
deepSkyObjects.summary: A grand-design spiral galaxy in the nearby M81 group.

deepSkyObjects.name: Cigar Galaxy
deepSkyObjects.summary: A starburst galaxy whose central region is forming stars at an intense rate.

deepSkyObjects.name: Large Magellanic Cloud
deepSkyObjects.summary: A satellite galaxy of the Milky Way and one of the closest galaxies to Earth.

deepSkyObjects.name: Small Magellanic Cloud
deepSkyObjects.summary: A nearby dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way.

deepSkyObjects.name: 47 Tucanae
deepSkyObjects.summary: A massive globular cluster near the Small Magellanic Cloud in the southern sky.

deepSkyObjects.name: Carina Nebula
deepSkyObjects.summary: A giant star-forming complex containing some of the Milky Way's most massive stars.

deepSkyObjects.name: Omega Centauri
deepSkyObjects.summary: The largest and brightest globular cluster visible from Earth.

worldProfiles.name: Earth
worldProfiles.highlight: The reference point for every comparison.

worldProfiles.name: Mars
worldProfiles.highlight: A cold desert with a day length surprisingly close to Earth's.

worldProfiles.name: Europa
worldProfiles.highlight: An icy ocean world with almost no atmosphere.

worldProfiles.name: Titan
worldProfiles.highlight: A frigid moon with a thicker atmosphere than Earth's.

worldProfiles.name: Proxima Centauri b
worldProfiles.highlight: The closest known potentially habitable exoplanet candidate.

worldProfiles.name: TRAPPIST-1 e
worldProfiles.highlight: An Earth-sized world in a compact multi-planet system.
